title: SODIS + household filtration + settling/flocculation units (method-level, no dosing)
context: Solar disinfection (SODIS), household filtration, and settling/flocculation are household water treatment methods that complement boiling and chemical treatment. At the method-level (conditions, technique, appropriate use) these are medium-risk topics. Any chemical dosing quantities (e.g., flocculant amounts) are explicitly excluded from these units and reserved for the HIGH-risk M2 task (content-017). These units build on the authoring pattern established by content-006.
objective: Author, source-cite, and get domain-reviewed WashUnit documents covering: (1) solar disinfection (SODIS) — conditions, bottle types, exposure duration, appropriate use cases; (2) household filtration — filter types and appropriate selection (ceramic, biosand, etc.) at the household level; and (3) settling and flocculation — the technique and when to use it, without any chemical dosing amounts, which are reserved for M2.
acceptanceCriteria: SODIS unit covers conditions (turbidity, bottle type, sunlight duration/UV dose) and technique at the method level; any dosing amounts for edge-condition chemical use are explicitly excluded and noted as covered in a HIGH-risk M2 unit
Filtration unit covers household filter types, selection, and use/maintenance without product/brand recommendations
Flocculation/settling unit covers the technique without specifying flocculant dosing quantities (those are split to content-017 per the risk-classification policy)
Every claim tied to a specific named authoritative source (WHO/UNICEF/CDC/Sphere) with retrieval date and verified license; no verbatim copied text; no logos/emblems; CI copy/similarity check passes
Each unit reviewed and approved by a WASH/public-health domain reviewer distinct from the author; PR-tied review-log entries written; riskClass is medium; no dosing instruction embedded
CI schema validation, citation-coverage, no-self-approval, readability, and copy/similarity gates all pass
output: Committed, domain-reviewed, source-cited WashUnit documents for SODIS, household filtration, and settling/flocculation at the method level (no dosing), with PR-tied review-log entries.
